title: [VPlan] Remove non-reductions after simplifications.
author: fhahn
description:
In some cases, we identify patterns as reductions, even though they can be simplified to a non-reduction.

Mark VPReductionPHIRecipe as not reading from memory & not having side-effects, to clean them up.

We also need to remove ComputeReductionResult VPInstructions with live-in arguments. This means there is actually no reduction, and we need to fold it to the live in. Otherwise we would incorrectly reduce the live-in.

tests.test_body:
define i1 @reduction_with_const_or(ptr %A, i8 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %or.red = phi i1 [ false, %entry ], [ %or.next, %loop ]
  %iv = phi i8 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %iv.ext = zext i8 %iv to i64
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %A, i64 %iv.ext
  store i64 %iv.ext, ptr %gep, align 8
  %or.next = or i1 %or.red, true
  %iv.next = add i8 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp eq i8 %iv, %n
  br i1 %exitcond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i1 %or.next
}
